description: An <each> body with MORE THAN ONE per-item root renders EVERY root, and the keyed reconciler treats the roots of one item as a single group. Three items x 2 roots = 6 nodes, interleaved hdr/row per item. Clicking the first row's delete button drops id=1 and removes BOTH of that item's roots, leaving the B and C groups intact and in order.
rationale: §17.7.2 requires 'at least one' per-item template element — it does not cap the count at one, and §10.8 grants N nodes per iteration explicitly for the Tier-0 accumulation form. Adopter issue #141: roots 2..N were built, wired, and then silently discarded by a createFn contract that returned exactly one Node. Pinning the RUNTIME half: N roots must reach the DOM, and a per-key removal must take the whole group with it.
